lines.en_lit: You must say this is the lamb

Rabbi Asi said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: A non-priest who set up the arrangement of wood is liable What should he do He dismantle it and then rearrange it. What good would this do for Rather, non-priest dismantle and a priest rearranges it.
Rabbi Zeira strongly objects And do you have any service that is valid if performed at night and is invalid by a non-priest?
And no Isn’t there of the limbs and the fats the end of the daytime service,

But isn’t there the removal of the ashes the start of the daytime service. that Rabbi Asi said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: sanctified his hands for the removal of the ashes, the next day, he need not sanctify as he already sanctified at the start of the service. If so, difficult.

Rather, when it was stated, this is how it was stated. Rabbi Asi said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: A non-priest who arranges the two logs is liable since it is a daytime service. Rava strongly objects to this: But if so, should require a lottery; it must have escaped that which is taught who was privileged to removal of the ashes was privileged with setting up the arrangement of wood two logs.

Is that to say that a daytime service requires a lottery a nighttime service does not require a lottery? Isn’t there the limbs and the fats is the end of the daytime service, But isn’t there the removal of the ashes due to the incident that occurred,

Is that to say that daytime service and a non-priest liable the death penalty requires a lottery, a non-priest not liable the death penalty does not require a lottery? But isn’t there slaughtering Slaughtering is different, it is the beginning of the service
Mar Zutra, and some say Rav Ashi, said: But didn’t we learn it is not so The appointed said to them: Go out and see if the time for slaughtering has arrived. does not teach the time for the two logs
teach that which has no rectification It does not teach something that has rectification
And some say Rabbi Zeira strongly objects to And do you have any service that is followed by a different service, and invalid by a non-priest?
